results.claim: Validator independently benchmarked kernel with conservative L2 flush methodology, diverging 1.43x from proposal baseline but still passing kill criterion
Validator independently verified 512-step accuracy stability and non-accumulation of BF16 error
Validator independently confirmed Marlin max_abs 7.81e-3 and Triton bit-identical correctness across all batch sizes
Validator independently determined FP32 workspace + pipeline depth degradation as root cause of 13-17% regression
Campaign exhausted after 3 rounds with 8.11% combined E2E at BS=32
Campaign summary with remaining bottleneck ceiling
GPU: 4x NVIDIA L40S, 45 GiB each, using CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0
Champion-1 used f=0.148 while simultaneously citing 7,118 ms FP8 CUTLASS total (internally inconsistent: 7118/94487=0.0753)
Champion-1 projected 5.67% E2E at BS=32 using the inflated f=0.148
Champion-1 conceded corrected f=0.0753, dropping E2E from 5.7% to 1.8-2.4%
Champion-1 fabricated 68.5 us/call figure not traceable to any source artifact
Actual per-call averages are 31.8 us (variant A) and 46.1 us (variant B), weighted ~34.8 us -- not 68.5 us
Champion-3 identified cold times ~2x inflated and challenged 68.5 us as double-count
Champion-1 cited f=0.148 as 'grounded from profiling' and projected 6.5% E2E in Phase A
Champion-1 summary table repeated f=0.148 and 4.7-6.5% as official grounded range
Both Round 1 candidates failed: OP-002-splitk (EVT blocks, 1.04x) and OP-001-relu2 (0.58% < 1.0% threshold)
Debate scoring gave OP-002-splitk 6/10 feasibility despite zero empirical split-K benchmarks
Delegate-3a identified StreamK + SplitK interaction and shared_dn prime K factorization
Delegate-2a corrected bottleneck_analysis.md error: empty experts are already excluded, not reading weights
Delegate-3a computed per-shape ceilings differentiating variant A (2.54-2.67x) from variant B (1.40x)
Delegate-1a provided verified tile config mapping but Champion-1 never cited it
Champion-1 conceded corrected f=0.0753 in rebuttal
OP-003-bf16 discovered regime-shift: BF16 state fits L2, FP32 overflows
Round 2 context: Round 1 exhausted, exploring new approaches informed by failures
All 3 Round 2 candidates target different components, enabling additive E2E up to 8.6-11%
Split-K lessons: workspace overhead, pipeline depth, StreamK baseline efficiency
OP-003-bf16 E2E results across all batch sizes
OP-003-bf16 E2E at BS=8
OP-001-ext E2E at BS=8
OP-001-ext E2E at BS=32: +1.31%
Combined E2E verified by paired 20-iter sweep: BS=32 +8.11%
OP-002-splitk failed: EVT blocks kGemmSplitKParallel, tile-M halving only 1.04x
OP-001-relu2 failed at BS=32 avg 0.58%, P50 1.22%, with only 5 iterations
OP-002-v2 regresses 13-17% on all shapes
Single-CTA BW 44 GB/s, 13.6x below proposal. Even theoretical max gives 1.47%.
Triton loses to cuBLAS due to larger K accumulating per-iteration overhead vs Qwen
BF16 state enables L2 residency regime shift at BS=32
Split-K lessons: workspace overhead, pipeline depth, StreamK baseline efficiency
Triton vs cuBLAS depends on K dimension: 1.07x at K=896, 0.49-0.81x at K=2688-4096
Single-CTA BW on L40S is 44 GB/s, 13.6x below 600 GB/s assumption
f-value accuracy critical: debate caught f=0.148 -> f=0.0753 correction
E2E from kernel elimination, not speedup; Triton fused time similar to 2-kernel baseline
Round 1 vs Round 2: scope + statistical confidence drove improvement
NemotronH: 52 layers, Mamba + MoE + Attention, ~3B active per token
Round 1 overcounted FP8 CUTLASS by including prefill variants; true decode share 8.4% not 14.8%
Combined 8.11% vs individual sum 7.62%, super-additive by 0.49%
